decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hot spot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak chip temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementations, though this is a simulation.
